title: papaver lapponicum
intro: Papaver lapponicum, also known as the Arctic poppy, is a perennial plant species native to the Arctic tundra regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Papaver lapponicum include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
